steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Start with $$(6t^4-6t^2+4t-2)-(5t^4-3t^3+7t^2-2).$$ Subtracting the second parentheses changes every sign:
$$6t^4-6t^2+4t-2-5t^4+3t^3-7t^2+2.$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The $t^4$ terms give $t^4$. The cubic term is $3t^3$. The $t^2$ terms give $-6t^2-7t^2=-13t^2$. The linear term is $4t$, and the constants cancel. So the expression is $$t^4+3t^3-13t^2+4t.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: That expression matches choice A exactly. Choice D is close but has an extra $-4$ constant. Choice A is correct.
